summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary snapshot with git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walk and excludes dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files and walkFiles, excludes allowed roots
evidence.why: Core boundary snapshot implementation covers git and fallback file discovery.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z for tracked/cached/other files
evidence.why: Implements git-backed file discovery including untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively falls back through project tree excluding standard dirs
evidence.why: Non-git boundary fallback coverage.

evidence.summary: excludedDir filters .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Standard exclusions prevent noise in boundary snapshots.

evidence.summary: Runtime takes before/after boundary snapshots and diffs for mutations
evidence.why: Execution boundary audit detects target project mutations.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots detects added/modified/deleted file changes
evidence.why: Change detection for boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Test evidence for git untracked file detection.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Test evidence for gitignored file detection.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Test evidence for non-git boundary behavior.

evidence.summary: Boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test evidence for boundary enforcement over agent results.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations add BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and fail PRD readiness
evidence.why: Boundary violations block demand progress.

evidence.summary: Evidence agent protocols define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles
evidence.why: Evidence agent architecture defines multi-agent verification.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates strict JSON-only agent instructions
evidence.why: Agent prompt structure enforces evidence protocol compliance.

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines forbidden project mutation and allowed write roots
evidence.why: Boundary rules are explicit in dispatch plan.

assumptions: The git working tree is dirty but has not been tested with real Claude -p agents.
Boundary detection works in tests but may behave differently under full-tool stress.
Excluded directories list covers common cases but may miss project-specific ignores.
risks: Full-tool agent could create files outside git ls-files coverage without detection if allowed roots are misconfigured.
Gitignored files outside standard exclusions might not be detected in all git configurations.
Non-git fallback walk may be slow for large project trees.

result.notes: Boundary snapshot implementation matches acceptance criteria. Tests cover git tracked/untracked/gitignored and non-git cases. Runtime enforcement adds blockers for mutations. Assumption: needs real Claude agent stress test to confirm full-tool boundary holds.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is factually grounded and properly separates assumptions from verified project facts.

evidence.summary: gitFiles function uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to discover tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim that git-backed file discovery covers untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles function recursively walks project tree excluding standard directories
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about non-git boundary fallback coverage.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines gitFiles and walkFiles, excludes allowed roots
evidence.why: Confirms core boundary snapshot implementation matches acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots function detects added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: Verifies change detection mechanism for boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Runtime captures before/after boundary snapshots and diffs for mutations
evidence.why: Confirms runtime boundary audit detects target project mutations.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations add BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and fail PRD readiness
evidence.why: Verifies enforcement mechanism blocks demand progress on violations.

evidence.summary: Test case 'git-backed boundary blocks untracked project mutation' creates untracked file and verifies detection
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about git untracked file detection evidence.

evidence.summary: Test case 'git-backed boundary blocks gitignored project mutation' creates gitignored file outside standard exclusions
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about gitignored file detection evidence.

evidence.summary: Test case 'non-git boundary blocks project mutation and ignores artifacts' verifies non-git behavior
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about non-git boundary behavior evidence.

evidence.summary: Test case 'boundary blocks project mutation even when agent results pass' confirms enforcement priority
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about boundary enforcement over agent results.

evidence.summary: Evidence agent protocols define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with specific schemas
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about evidence agent architecture.

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines forbidden project mutation and allowed write roots including dispatch artifact root
evidence.why: Confirms boundary rules are explicit in dispatch plan.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt function generates strict JSON-only agent instructions with protocol boundary
evidence.why: Verifies agent prompt structure enforces evidence protocol compliance.

assumptions: Explorer's three assumptions are properly segregated from evidence and not presented as facts.
Evidence paths and line numbers are usable and point to actual project code.
Missing data is properly reflected as assumptions rather than gaps in evidence.
risks: Explorer evidence is sufficient for current PRD readiness but real Claude agent stress testing remains an assumption.

Acceptance criteria mention 'exclude only the dispatch artifact root' but evidence shows standard exclusions (.git, node_modules, etc.) are also applied - this is reasonable but not explicitly stated in acceptance criteria.

result.notes: Explorer evidence is factually grounded with 15 verifiable project code/test references. Assumptions are clearly separated and identified. Evidence paths are usable and accurate. One minor clarification: acceptance criteria state 'exclude only the dispatch artifact root' but implementation also applies standard exclusions (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) which is appropriate and tested. The claim that boundary snapshots cover git ls-files plus fallback walk and exclude dispatch artifact root is verified by evidence.
